### Post by Plasma_NZ on 2008-04-30
Ok.. running hardy heron - had this problem for ages.. tried most solutions, nothings helped..

Running a 8500GT Nvidia
Running a SamSung 931bw LCD 19nch

I cant get it to run @ 70hz, it's stuck @ 60 hertz no matter what i try in the xorg.conf -

here's my xorg.conf

Please help find me the fix..!

```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
```

### Post by hermes0710 on 2008-04-30
You monitor's optimum resolution is @ 60Hz, why change it anyway?

### Post by Plasma_NZ on 2008-04-30

### Post by danwood76 on 2008-04-30
What drivers are you using?

If your using proprietry you should try and use the nvidia config tool to setup your resolution, the xorg.conf is slowly being phased out I think.

from a terminal:
```

sudo nvidia-settings

```

### Post by danwood76 on 2008-05-30
[quote=Samsung Spec]
Optimum resolution 1440 X 900@60 Hz
[/quote]
60Hz is optimum (native) for your LCD, is there any specific reason your trying to get it to 75Hz?
I doubt you will notice the difference even if you do, it may even look worse as its not optimum.
